steps.title: Identify the main purpose signal
steps.content: The narrator goes to the bus stop specifically to watch gym-goers on stair machines: “there ain’t nothing funnier than that.” He frames the sight as entertainment, calling it The About to Pass Out Show. The main purpose is to describe something the narrator finds amusing.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — To give a reason why the narrator is excited to start middle school: Middle school is mentioned in the setup but is not the subject of the passage; the text never expresses excitement about school. ✗
  • Choice B — To describe an activity that the narrator finds amusing: The narrator explicitly calls watching gym-goers funny and treats it like a comedy show — directly matches the passage’s focus. ✓
  • Choice C — To explain a problem that the narrator has overcome: No problem or challenge appears anywhere in the text. ✗
  • Choice D — To discuss a movie that the narrator saw in a theater: “The About to Pass Out Show” is the narrator’s invented label for the gym scene, not a real movie; there is no theater. ✗
That matches Choice B — To describe an activity that the narrator finds amusing.
